label: WEATHER
text: Cold rain turns the city into a charcoal smear. Your clothes cling. Your fingers go numb. Somewhere nearby, infected shuffle through puddles with patient feet.

label: SCAVENGE
text: An overturned delivery truck blocks an alley. Most crates are smashed, but one still holds powdered soup and batteries. The alley is narrow enough to become a trap.

label: NOISE
text: A distant explosion rolls over the rooftops. Birds scatter. Infected turn toward the sound, then toward every other sound, including your breathing.

label: ENCOUNTER
text: A child steps from behind a mailbox with a kitchen knife held backward. They ask if you know where the evacuation buses went. Their voice is too calm.

label: HUNGER
text: The can looked sealed. It was not. You lose an hour behind a pharmacy counter, sweating through cramps and promising the universe you will read labels now.
